brief.title: American Airlines
brief.scope: Contributed to the redesign and maintenance of the American Airlines portal, an aviation industry platform, working across the full stack alongside Ruby on Rails and MongoDB-backed services.
brief.problem: The portal needed a redesign and continued maintenance to support a large volume of users with reliable, responsive functionality, while integrating tightly with a Ruby on Rails backend and MongoDB data layer.
key_challenges: Maintaining a high-traffic, business-critical aviation portal during redesign efforts.
Coordinating frontend changes with a Ruby on Rails backend and MongoDB data layer.
Ensuring responsive design and reliability across a broad user base.
key_contributions: Implemented redesigned UI features using AngularJS and JavaScript within a Ruby on Rails application.
Maintained responsive layouts using HTML5, CSS3, and Less.
Supported unit testing (Jasmine, Mocha, Chai) to validate redesign and maintenance work.
Collaborated with backend engineers on MongoDB-backed data features (using Robo3T for data inspection).
technical_focus: Frontend integration within a Ruby on Rails application.
Responsive web design for a high-traffic aviation portal.
Legacy AngularJS maintenance and testing practices.
brief.suggested_diagrams: Redesign rollout flow across portal sections. Frontend-to-Rails-to-MongoDB data flow. Responsive design breakpoints for the portal.
system_impact: Supported reliable operation of a business-critical portal for a major airline.
Delivered redesigned features without disrupting high-traffic user flows.
Reinforced testing practices that reduced regression risk during ongoing maintenance.
learnings: Working on a high-traffic, business-critical platform sharpened my sense of risk management in frontend changes — every deployment needed to consider scale and reliability. This experience reinforced how closely frontend architecture decisions are tied to backend data models in full-stack environments.
